# terrible_splunk_es_splunk_data_input_monitor (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The file or directory path to monitor on the system.
- `state` (String) Add or remove a data source.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `blacklist` (String) Specify a regular expression for a file path. The file path that matches this regular expression is not indexed.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `check_index` (Boolean) If set to `true`, the index value is checked to ensure that it is the name of a valid index.
- `check_path` (Boolean) If set to `true`, the name value is checked to ensure that it exists.
- `crc_salt` (String) A string that modifies the file tracking identity for files in this input. The magic value <SOURCE> invokes special behavior (see admin documentation).
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Indicates if input monitoring is disabled.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `followTail` (Boolean) If set to `true`, files that are seen for the first time is read from the end.
- `host` (String) The value to populate in the host field for events from this data input.
- `host_regex` (String) Specify a regular expression for a file path. If the path for a file matches this regular expression, the captured value is used to populate the host field for events from this data input. The regular expression must have one capture group.
- `host_segment` (Number) Use the specified slash-separate segment of the filepath as the host field value.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ignore_older_than` (String) Specify a time value. If the modification time of a file being monitored falls outside of this rolling time window, the file is no longer being monitored.
- `index` (String) Which index events from this input should be stored in. Defaults to default.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `recursive` (Boolean) Setting this to false prevents monitoring of any subdirectories encountered within this data input.
- `rename_source` (String) The value to populate in the source field for events from this data input. The same source should not be used for multiple data inputs.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `sourcetype` (String) The value to populate in the sourcetype field for incoming events.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `time_before_close` (Number) When Splunk software reaches the end of a file that is being read, the file is kept open for a minimum of the number of seconds specified in this value. After this period has elapsed, the file is checked again for more data.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `whitelist` (String) Specify a regular expression for a file path. Only file paths that match this regular expression are indexed.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
